flows.label: f(a0) — Right b' or Left e'

notes.text: Pattern match short-circuits:\nLeft skips f entirely.
Type widens, value preserved bit-identical.\nThis is what 'error preserved verbatim' means.
Whatever f returns (Right b' or Left e')\nis forwarded UNCHANGED — bind never\ninspects or rewraps f's result.

tooltips.ea: Either e a — the input.  Either Right a0 (success carrying a value of type a) or Left e0 (failure carrying an error value of type e).
tooltips.f: (a ⟶ Either e b) — the continuation.  Invoked ONLY on the Right branch.  Skipped entirely when ea is Left.
tooltips.matchEa:
Pattern match on the sum-type constructor.
  Right a0 → a0 : a flows directly into f (no separate unwrap step).
  Left  e0 → e0 : e flows directly into widen; the right branch is skipped.
Exactly one branch fires per invocation; the runtime never executes both.
tooltips.apply_f: Apply f(a0).  f returns Either e b directly (Right b' or Left e').  bind does NOT inspect f's result — whatever f returned is forwarded unchanged.  This is what makes bind compositional: a Left arising inside f propagates exactly the same way as a Left arising before f.
tooltips.widen: Re-tag the Left constructor: the runtime VALUE Left e0 stays bit-identical, but the static type widens from Left e to Either e b so it unifies with the Right branch's output type at the merge.  No allocation, no transformation — only the type changes.
tooltips.joinResult: Merge node — both branches produce a value of the SAME type Either e b.  Exactly one inflow fires per invocation; the merge is a control-flow join, not a runtime choice.
tooltips.ec: Either e b — the single result of bind.  Carries either f's output (when ea was Right) or the original error e0 (when ea was Left).

diagram.title: Either bind — also called: andThen / and_then, Bind, flatMap, >>=
diagram.name: bind
